channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump 2.0 has a Cabinet and executive branch of different positions and eclectic personalities
item.description: Donald Trump’s personnel choices for his new Cabinet and White House reflect his signature positions on immigration and trade but also a range of viewpoints and backgrounds that raise questions about what ideological anchors might guide his Oval Office encore.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: New Arizona Democrat weighs in on her win and Harris’s loss
item.description: Arizona Rep.-elect Yassamin Ansari (D) shared why she thinks she won in her swing state but Vice President Harris didn’t, while also weighing in on immigration as President-elect Trump prepares his deportation crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Sanctuary states, cities should explain to DOGE why they deserve federal money: MTG
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, R-Ga., on Sunday laid out areas that could face the chopping block under the subcommittee’s plan to cut government spending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s deportation vow alarms Texas construction industry
item.description: Texas builders warn mass deportations of undocumented migrants could devastate the construction industry, threatening housing and infrastructure work in one of the nation’s fastest-growing states.
item.dc:creator: Julian Aguilar, MPR News

item.title: Fred Harris, former Democratic senator from Oklahoma and presidential candidate, dies at 94
item.description: Former Sen. Fred Harris, D-Okla., died Saturday at age 94, his wife said. Harris is remembered for championing Democratic Party reforms in the 1960s.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Trump’s picks for CDC, FDA and surgeon general
item.description: In a flurry of picks on Friday evening, Trump named three choices for top health jobs. Together they would help the incoming president shift the priorities of agencies that are key to public health.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Brooke Rollins, Trump's pick for agriculture secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former White House aide Brooke Rollins to lead the Department of Agriculture in his second administration. Here are some things to know about Trump’s choice and the agency that Rollins would lead if she is confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Trump's Project 2025 denials, he is tapping its authors and influencers for key roles
item.description: During the campaign, President-elect Donald Trump had hailed what would become Project 2025 as a conservative roadmap for “exactly what our movement will do.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs. Trump was bruised and hampered by internal squabbles during his initial term in office.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary, the last of his picks to lead executive agencies and another choice from within his established circle of advisers and allies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Texan Brooke Rollins as agriculture secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Brooke Rollins, who served in his first administration as director of the Office of American Innovation and acting director of the Domestic Policy Council, as his agriculture secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at how some of Trump's picks to lead health agencies could help carry out Kennedy's overhaul
item.description: The team that President-elect Donald Trump has selected to lead federal health agencies in his second administration includes a retired congressman, a surgeon and a former talk-show host.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas AG sues Dallas for decriminalizing marijuana
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has sued the city of Dallas over a ballot measure that decriminalizes possession of small amounts of marijuana, arguing it violates state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names Seb Gorka, Alex Wong to be senior national security staff
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped his first term alums Alex Wong and Sebastian Gorka to join his national security team for his second go-around.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: An influx of outsiders and money turns Montana Republican, culminating in a Senate triumph
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — Democrats' crushing loss in Montana's nationally important U.S. Senate race settled a fierce political debate over whether a surge of newcomers in the past decade favored Republicans — and if one of the new arrivals could even take high office.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates former Texas lawmaker Scott Turner to lead Housing and Urban Development
item.description: Turner served in the Texas House for four years and was also executive director of the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii And Renzo Downey, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump taps NFL player Scott Turner as head of HUD
item.description: Turner serves as the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at America First Policy Institute, a Trump-aligned think tank.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Scott Turner as secretary for Department of Housing and Urban Development
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated former NFL cornerback Scott Turner and Texas state representative as the secretary of the Department of Housing and Urban Development.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names Chavez-DeRemer for Labor secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer (R-Ore.), to serve as Labor secretary in his new administration. Chavez-DeRemer had the backing of the Teamsters before she was officially picked by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Project 2025 author Russell Vought to lead budget office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump tapped Project 2025 co-author Russell Vought to serve as the director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) during his second administration. Vought served as Trump's OMB deputy director during his first term in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: NJ Rep. Gottheimer vows to fight congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) — Rep. Josh Gottheimer has been a staunch opponent of congestion pricing since its introduction. Now, with a new plan set in motion by New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump encounters Senate GOP limits with failed Gaetz AG push
item.description: President-elect Trump's ill-fated nomination of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to run the Justice Department marked an early sign of his potential limits in moving his nominees and dealing with Senate Republicans, giving him less wiggle room to get his Cabinet in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota leads nation in voter turnout, but Wisconsin results loom
item.description: The state canvassing board met in St. Paul Thursday to certify the results of the 2024 election. Wisconsin will certify its results on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) political future is up in the air following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Minnesota Lottery needs to tighten up how it regulates retailers
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of the Legislative Auditor reports that the Minnesota State Lottery generally complies with requirements, but has room to improve when it comes to how it manages retailers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: Matt Gaetz has withdrawn as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general following scrutiny over a federal sex trafficking investigation. The Florida Republican made the announcement Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Matt Gaetz withdraws from attorney general nomination
item.description: Former Florida congressman Matt Gaetz said he is no longer seeking the position of U.S. attorney general Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida legislators won't have special session for condo concerns
item.description: Florida condo owners are facing a conundrum. Units for sale are sitting on the market longer and owners who want to stay are facing rising costs.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Anti-abortion groups have a to-do list for Trump
item.description: With Trump set to begin another term and Republicans in control of Congress, the anti-abortion movement is hoping to continue the gains it made during his first time in office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks business executive Linda McMahon to lead the Education Department
item.description: McMahon is a professional wrestling business magnate and co-chair of President-elect Donald Trump's transition team. She has limited experience working with K-12 public schools.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
